metadata.name: python-tds
metadata.summary: Python DBAPI driver for MSSQL using pure Python TDS (Tabular Data Stream) protocol implementation

modules.name: pytds.utils
documentation:
This module contains generic utility functions which don't have dependencies on any
other modules.

functions.name: pytds.utils.exponential_backoff
documentation:
Perform work with exponential backoff if work fails.
Will raise TimeoutError if `max_time_sec` is exceeded.
Work handler receives time limit in seconds for the attempt,
it should use it to setup timeout for it's operations.
The `ex_handler` is called every time work raises exception,
if `ex_handler` can raise exception itself to stop

functions.name: pytds.utils.parse_server
documentation: Split server name in MSSQL format (host\instance) into server host and instance

functions.name: pytds.utils.ver_to_int
documentation: Convert version string into 32-bit integer format

modules.name: pytds.row_strategies
documentation:
This module implements various row strategies.
E.g. row strategy that generated dictionaries or named tuples for rows.

functions.name: pytds.row_strategies.tuple_row_strategy
documentation: Tuple row strategy, rows returned as tuples, default

functions.name: pytds.row_strategies.list_row_strategy
documentation: List row strategy, rows returned as lists

functions.name: pytds.row_strategies.dict_row_strategy
documentation: Dict row strategy, rows returned as dictionaries

functions.name: pytds.row_strategies.is_valid_identifier
documentation: Returns true if given name can be used as an identifier in Python, otherwise returns false.

functions.name: pytds.row_strategies.namedtuple_row_strategy
documentation:
Namedtuple row strategy, rows returned as named tuples

Column names that are not valid Python identifiers will be replaced
with col<number>_

functions.name: pytds.row_strategies.recordtype_row_strategy
documentation:
Recordtype row strategy, rows returned as recordtypes

Column names that are not valid Python identifiers will be replaced
with col<number>_

documentation:
This class defines an interface for cursor classes.
It is implemented by MARS and non-MARS cursor classes.

classes.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.__iter__
documentation:
Return self to make cursors compatibile with Python iteration
protocol.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.get_proc_outputs
documentation:
If stored procedure has result sets and OUTPUT parameters use this method
after you processed all result sets to get values of the OUTPUT parameters.
\:return: A list of output parameter values.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.callproc
documentation:
Call a stored procedure with the given name.

```
param procname

The name of the procedure to call

type procname

str

keyword parameters

The optional parameters for the procedure

type parameters

sequence
```

Note: If stored procedure has OUTPUT parameters and result sets this
method will not return values for OUTPUT parameters, you should
call get_proc_outputs to get values for OUTPUT parameters.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.return_value
documentation: Alias to {func}`get_proc_return_status`

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.spid
documentation:
MSSQL Server's session ID (SPID)

It can be used to correlate connections between client and server logs.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.get_proc_return_status
documentation:
Last executed stored procedure's return value

Returns integer value returned by `RETURN` statement from last executed stored procedure.
If no value was not returned or no stored procedure was executed return `None`.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.cancel
documentation: Cancel currently executing statement or stored procedure call

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.close
documentation: Closes the cursor. The cursor is unusable from this point.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.execute
documentation:
Execute an SQL query

Optionally query can be executed with parameters.
To make parametrized query use `%s` in the query to denote a parameter
and pass a tuple with parameter values, e.g.:

```
execute("select %s, %s", (1,2))
```

This will execute query replacing first `%s` with first parameter value - 1,
and second `%s` with second parameter value -2.

Another option is to use named parameters with passing a dictionary, e.g.:

```
execute("select %(param1)s, %(param2)s", {param1=1, param2=2})
```

Both those ways of passing parameters is safe from SQL injection attacks.

This function does not return results of the execution.
Use {func}`fetchone` or similar to fetch results.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.executemany
documentation: Execute same SQL query multiple times for each parameter set in the `params_seq` list.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.execute_scalar
documentation:
This method executes SQL query then returns first column of first row or the
result.

Query can be parametrized, see {func}`execute` method for details.

This method is useful if you want just a single value, as in:

```
conn.execute_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees')
```

This method works in the same way as `iter(conn).next()[0]`.
Remaining rows, if any, can still be iterated after calling this
method.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.nextset
documentation:
Move to next recordset in batch statement, all rows of current recordset are
discarded if present.

```
returns

true if successful or None when there are no more recordsets
```

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.rowcount
documentation:
Number of rows affected by previous statement

:returns: -1 if this information was not supplied by the server

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.description
documentation: Cursor description, see http://legacy.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0249/#description

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.set_stream
documentation:
This function can be used to efficiently receive values which can be very large, e.g. `TEXT`, `VARCHAR(MAX)`, `VARBINARY(MAX)`.

When streaming is not enabled, values are loaded to memory as they are received from server and
once entire row is loaded, it is returned.

With this function streaming receiver can be specified via `stream` parameter which will receive chunks of the data
as they are received. For each received chunk driver will call stream's write method.
For example this can be used to save value of a field into a file, or to
proces value as it is being received.

For string fields chunks are represented as unicode strings.
For binary fields chunks are represented as `bytes` strings.

Example usage:

```
cursor.execute("select N'very large field'")
cursor.set_stream(0, StringIO())
row = cursor.fetchone()
# now row[0] contains instance of a StringIO object which was gradually
# filled with output from server for first column.
```

```
param column_idx

Zero based index of a column for which to setup streaming receiver

type column_idx

int

param stream

Stream object that will be receiving chunks of data via it's  method.
```

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.fetchone
documentation:
Fetch next row.

Returns row using currently configured factory, or `None` if there are no more rows

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.fetchmany
documentation:
Fetch next N rows

```
param size

Maximum number of rows to return, default value is cursor.arraysize

returns

List of rows
```

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.fetchall
documentation:
Fetch all remaining rows

Do not use this if you expect large number of rows returned by the server,
since this method will load all rows into memory.  It is more efficient
to load and process rows by iterating over them.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.setinputsizes
documentation: This method does nothing, as permitted by DB-API specification.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.setoutputsize
documentation: This method does nothing, as permitted by DB-API specification.

methods.name: pytds.cursor.BaseCursor.copy_to
documentation:
*Experimental*. Efficiently load data to database from file using `BULK INSERT` operation

```
param file

Source file-like object, should be in csv format. Specify
either this or data, not both.

param table_or_view

Destination table or view in the database

type table_or_view

str
```

Optional parameters:

```
keyword sep

Separator used in csv file

type sep

str

keyword columns

List of  objects or column names in target
table to insert to. SQL Server will do some conversions, so these
may not have to match the actual table definition exactly.
If not provided will insert into all columns assuming nvarchar(4000)
NULL for all columns.
If only the column name is provided, the type is assumed to be
nvarchar(4000) NULL.
If rows are given with file, you cannot specify non-string data
types.
If rows are given with data, the values must be a type supported by
the serializer for the column in tds_types.

type columns

list

keyword check_constraints

Check table constraints for incoming data

type check_constraints

bool

keyword fire_triggers

Enable or disable triggers for table

type fire_triggers

bool

keyword keep_nulls

If enabled null values inserted as-is, instead of
inserting default value for column

type keep_nulls

bool

keyword kb_per_batch

Kilobytes per batch can be used to optimize performance, see MSSQL
server documentation for details

type kb_per_batch

int

keyword rows_per_batch

Rows per batch can be used to optimize performance, see MSSQL
server documentation for details

type rows_per_batch

int

keyword order

The ordering of the data in source table. List of columns with ASC or DESC suffix.
E.g. ['order_id ASC', 'name DESC']
Can be used to optimize performance, see MSSQL server documentation for details

type order

list

keyword tablock

Enable or disable table lock for the duration of bulk load

keyword schema

Name of schema for table or view, if not specified default schema will be used

keyword null_string

String that should be interpreted as a NULL when
reading the CSV file. Has no meaning if using data instead of file.

keyword data

The data to insert as an iterable of rows, which are
iterables of values. Specify either data parameter or file parameter but not both.
```

This class represents a base database cursor, which is used to issue queries
and fetch results from a database connection.
There are two actual cursor classes: one for MARS connections and one
for non-MARS connections.

classes.name: pytds.cursor.NonMarsCursor
documentation:
This class represents a non-MARS database cursor, which is used to issue queries
and fetch results from a database connection.

Non-MARS connections allow only one cursor to be active at a given time.

classes.name: pytds.cursor._MarsCursor

methods.name: pytds.cursor._MarsCursor.close
documentation: Closes the cursor. The cursor is unusable from this point.

This class represents a MARS database cursor, which is used to issue queries
and fetch results from a database connection.

MARS connections allow multiple cursors to be active at the same time.

modules.name: pytds.sspi
documentation: This module implements wrapper for Windows SSPI API

functions.name: pytds.sspi.enum_security_packages
documentation: class SspiSecBuffer(object):
def __init__(self, type, buflen=4096):
self._buf = create_string_buffer(int(buflen))
self._desc = SecBuffer()
self._desc.cbBuffer = buflen
self._desc.BufferType = type
self._desc.pvBuffer = cast(self._buf, PVOID)
class SspiSecBuffers(object):
def __init__(self):
self._desc = SecBufferDesc()
self._desc.ulVersion = SECBUFFER_VERSION
self._descrs = (SecBuffer * 8)()
self._desc.pBuffers = self._descrs
def append(self, buf):
if len(self._descrs) <= self._desc.cBuffers:
newdescrs = (SecBuffer * (len(self._descrs) * 2))(*self._descrs)
self._descrs = newdescrs
self._desc.pBuffers = newdescrs
self._descrs[self._desc.cBuffers] = buf._desc
self._desc.cBuffers += 1

Type of current packet

Possible values are TDS_QUERY, TDS_LOGIN, etc.

methods.name: pytds.tds_reader._TdsReader.stream_finished
documentation:
Verifies whether the current response packet stream has finished reading.
If the function returns True, it indicates that you should invoke the begin_response
method to initiate the reading of the next stream.
:return:

methods.name: pytds.tds_reader._TdsReader.read_fast
documentation:
Faster version of read

Instead of returning sliced buffer it returns reference to internal
buffer and the offset to this buffer.

```
param size

Number of bytes to read

returns

Tuple of bytes buffer, and offset in this buffer
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_reader._TdsReader.get_collation
documentation: Reads {class}`Collation` object from stream

methods.name: pytds.tds_reader._TdsReader.begin_response
documentation:
This method should be called first before reading anything.
It will read first response packet and return its metadata, after that
read methods can be called to read contents of the response packet stream until it ends.

methods.name: pytds.tds_reader._TdsReader._read_packet
documentation:
Reads next TDS packet from the underlying transport
Can only be called when transport's read pointer is at the beginning
of the packet.

methods.name: pytds.tds_reader._TdsReader.read_whole_packet
documentation:
Reads single packet and returns bytes payload of the packet

Can only be called when transport's read pointer is at the beginning
of the packet.

TDS stream reader

Provides stream-like interface for TDS packeted stream.
Also provides convinience methods to decode primitive data like
different kinds of integers etc.

Provides convinience methods for writing primitive data types.

This class represents root TDS connection
if MARS is used it can have multiple sessions represented by _TdsSession class
if MARS is not used it would have single _TdsSession instance

modules.name: pytds
documentation: DB-SIG compliant module for communicating with MS SQL servers

functions.name: pytds._get_servers_deque
documentation:
Returns deque of servers for given tuple of servers and
database name.
This deque have active server at the begining, if first server
is not accessible at the moment the deque will be rotated,
second server will be moved to the first position, thirt to the
second position etc, and previously first server will be moved
to the last position.
This allows to remember last successful server between calls
to connect function.

functions.name: pytds.connect
documentation:
Opens connection to the database

```
keyword dsn

SQL server host and instance: <host>[<instance>]

type dsn

string

keyword failover_partner

secondary database host, used if primary is not accessible

type failover_partner

string

keyword database

the database to initially connect to

type database

string

keyword user

database user to connect as

type user

string

keyword password

user's password

type password

string

keyword timeout

query timeout in seconds, default 0 (no timeout)

type timeout

int

keyword login_timeout

timeout for connection and login in seconds, default 15

type login_timeout

int

keyword as_dict

whether rows should be returned as dictionaries instead of tuples.

type as_dict

boolean

keyword appname

Set the application name to use for the connection

type appname

string

keyword port

the TCP port to use to connect to the server

type port

int

keyword tds_version

Maximum TDS version to use, should only be used for testing

type tds_version

int

keyword autocommit

Enable or disable database level autocommit

type autocommit

bool

keyword blocksize

Size of block for the TDS protocol, usually should not be used

type blocksize

int

keyword use_mars

Enable or disable MARS

type use_mars

bool

keyword auth

An instance of authentication method class, e.g. Ntlm or Sspi

keyword readonly

Allows to enable read-only mode for connection, only supported by MSSQL 2012,
earlier versions will ignore this parameter

type readonly

bool

keyword load_balancer

An instance of load balancer class to use, if not provided will not use load balancer

keyword use_tz

Provides timezone for naive database times, if not provided date and time will be returned
in naive format

keyword bytes_to_unicode

If true single byte database strings will be converted to unicode Python strings,
otherwise will return strings as bytes without conversion.

type bytes_to_unicode

bool

keyword row_strategy

strategy used to create rows, determines type of returned rows, can be custom or one of:
, , ,
, 

type row_strategy

function of list of column names returning row factory

keyword cafile

Name of the file containing trusted CAs in PEM format, if provided will enable TLS

type cafile

str

keyword validate_host

Host name validation during TLS connection is enabled by default, if you disable it you
will be vulnerable to MitM type of attack.

type validate_host

bool

keyword enc_login_only

Allows you to scope TLS encryption only to an authentication portion.  This means that
anyone who can observe traffic on your network will be able to see all your SQL requests and potentially modify
them.

type enc_login_only

bool

keyword use_sso

Enables SSO login, e.g. Kerberos using SSPI on Windows and kerberos package on other platforms.
Cannot be used together with auth parameter.

returns

An instance of 
```

modules.name: pytds.connection
documentation: This module implements DBAPI connection classes for both MARS and non-MARS variants

classes.name: pytds.connection.Connection
documentation:
This class defines interface for connection object according to DBAPI specification.
This interface is implemented by MARS and non-MARS connection classes.

classes.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.as_dict
documentation:
Instructs all cursors this connection creates to return results
as a dictionary rather than a tuple.

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.autocommit_state
documentation: An alias for `autocommit`, provided for compatibility with pymssql

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.set_autocommit
documentation: An alias for `autocommit`, provided for compatibility with ADO dbapi

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.autocommit
documentation: The current state of autocommit on the connection.

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.isolation_level
documentation:
Isolation level for transactions,
for possible values see {ref}`isolation-level-constants`

:::{seealso}
[SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL](http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms173763.aspx) in MSSQL documentation
:::

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.tds_version
documentation: Version of the TDS protocol that is being used by this connection

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.product_version
documentation: Version of the MSSQL server

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.commit
documentation: Commit transaction which is currently in progress.

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.rollback
documentation: Roll back transaction which is currently in progress.

methods.name: pytds.connection.BaseConnection.close
documentation:
Close connection to an MS SQL Server.

This function tries to close the connection and free all memory used.
It can be called more than once in a row. No exception is raised in
this case.

Base connection class.  It implements most of the common logic for
MARS and non-MARS connection classes.

classes.name: pytds.connection.MarsConnection

methods.name: pytds.connection.MarsConnection.cursor
documentation:
Return cursor object that can be used to make queries and fetch
results from the database.

MARS connection class, this object is created by calling {func}`connect`
with use_mars parameter set to False.

classes.name: pytds.connection.NonMarsConnection

methods.name: pytds.connection.NonMarsConnection.cursor
documentation:
Return cursor object that can be used to make queries and fetch
results from the database.

Non-MARS connection class, this object should be created by calling {func}`connect`
with use_mars parameter set to False.

documentation:
This class is deprecated since `ntlm-auth` package, on which it depends, is deprecated.
Instead use {class}`.SpnegoAuth`.

NTLM authentication, uses Python implementation (ntlm-auth)

For more information about NTLM authentication see <https://github.com/jborean93/ntlm-auth>

```
param user_name

User name

type user_name

str

param password

User password

type password

str

param ntlm_compatibility

NTLM compatibility level, default is 3(NTLMv2)

type ntlm_compatibility

int
```

classes.name: pytds.login.SpnegoAuth
documentation:
Authentication using Negotiate protocol, uses implementation provided pyspnego package

Takes same parameters as spnego.client function.

functions.name: pytds.tls.validate_host
pytds.tls.establish_channel
documentation: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd357559.aspx

functions.name: pytds.tls.revert_to_clear

methods.name: pytds.tls.EncryptedSocket.recv_into
documentation: def send(self, data):
while True:
try:
return self._tls_conn.send(data)
except OpenSSL.SSL.WantWriteError:
buf = self._tls_conn.bio_read(BUFSIZE)
self._transport.sendall(buf)

classes.name: pytds.tz.FixedOffsetTimezone
documentation: Fixed offset in minutes east from UTC.
A class building tzinfo objects for fixed-offset time zones.
Note that FixedOffset(0, "UTC") is a different way to build a
UTC tzinfo object.

modules.name: pytds.tds_base
documentation:
```
.. module:: tds_base
   :platform: Unix, Windows, MacOSX
   :synopsis: Various internal stuff
```

```
.. moduleauthor:: Mikhail Denisenko <denisenkom@gmail.com>
```

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.iterdecode
documentation:
Uses an incremental decoder to decode each chunk of string in iterable.
This function is a generator.

```
param iterable

Iterable object which yields raw data to be decoded.

param codec

An instance of a codec which will be used for decoding.
```

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.force_unicode
documentation: Convert input into a string.  If input is a byte array, it will be decoded using UTF8 decoder.

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.tds_quote_id
documentation:
Quote an identifier according to MSSQL rules

```
param ident

identifier to quote

returns

Quoted identifier
```

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.skipall
documentation:
Skips exactly size bytes in stm

If EOF is reached before size bytes are skipped
will raise {class}`ClosedConnectionError`

```
param stm

Stream to skip bytes in, should have read method
this read method can return less than requested
number of bytes.

param size

Number of bytes to skip.
```

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.read_chunks
documentation:
Reads exactly size bytes from stm and produces chunks

May call stm.read multiple times until required
number of bytes is read.
If EOF is reached before size bytes are read
will raise {class}`ClosedConnectionError`

```
param stm

Stream to read bytes from, should have read method,
this read method can return less than requested
number of bytes.

param size

Number of bytes to read.
```

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.readall
documentation:
Reads exactly size bytes from stm

May call stm.read multiple times until required
number of bytes read.
If EOF is reached before size bytes are read
will raise {class}`ClosedConnectionError`

```
param stm

Stream to read bytes from, should have read method
this read method can return less than requested
number of bytes.

param size

Number of bytes to read.

returns

Bytes buffer of exactly given size.
```

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.readall_fast
documentation:
Slightly faster version of readall, it reads no more than two chunks.
Meaning that it can only be used to read small data that doesn't span
more that two packets.

```
param stm

Stream to read from, should have read method.

param size

Number of bytes to read.

return

```

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.total_seconds
documentation:
Total number of seconds in timedelta object

Python 2.6 doesn't have total_seconds method, this function
provides a backport

functions.name: pytds.tds_base.tds7_crypt_pass
documentation:
Mangle password according to tds rules

```
param password

Password str

returns

Byte-string with encoded password
```

classes.name: pytds.tds_base.Param
documentation:
Describes typed parameter. Can be used to explicitly specify type of the parameter
in the parametrized query.

```
param name

Optional name of the parameter

type name

str

param type

Type of the parameter, e.g. 
```

classes.name: pytds.tds_base.Column

methods.name: pytds.tds_base.Column.choose_serializer
documentation: Chooses appropriate data type serializer for column's data type.

Describes table column. Can be used to define schema for bulk insert.

Following flags can be used for columns in `flags` parameter:

- {const}`.fNullable` - column can contain `NULL` values
- {const}`.fCaseSen` - column is case-sensitive
- {const}`.fReadWrite` - TODO document
- {const}`.fIdentity` - TODO document
- {const}`.fComputed` - TODO document

```
param name

Name of the column

type name

str

param type

Type of a column, e.g. 

param flags

Combination of flags for the column, multiple flags can be combined using binary or operator.
Possible flags are described above.
```

modules.name: pytds.tds_session
documentation: This module implements TdsSession class

classes.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.isolation_level
documentation:
Set transaction isolation level.
Will roll back current transaction if it has different isolation level.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.raise_db_exception
documentation:
Raises exception from last server message

This function will skip messages: The statement has been terminated

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.get_type_info
documentation:
Reads TYPE_INFO structure (<http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd358284.aspx>)

```
param curcol

An instance of  that will receive read information
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.tds7_process_result
documentation:
Reads and processes COLMETADATA stream

This stream contains a list of returned columns.
Stream format link: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd357363.aspx

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_param
documentation:
Reads and processes RETURNVALUE stream.

This stream is used to send OUTPUT parameters from RPC to client.
Stream format url: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd303881.aspx

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_cancel
documentation:
Process the incoming token stream until it finds
an end token DONE with the cancel flag set.
At that point the connection should be ready to handle a new query.

In case when no cancel request is pending this function does nothing.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_msg
documentation:
Reads and processes ERROR/INFO streams

Stream formats:

- ERROR: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd304156.aspx>
- INFO: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd303398.aspx>

```
param marker

TDS_ERROR_TOKEN or TDS_INFO_TOKEN
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_row
documentation:
Reads and handles ROW stream.

This stream contains list of values of one returned row.
Stream format url: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd357254.aspx

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_nbcrow
documentation:
Reads and handles NBCROW stream.

This stream contains list of values of one returned row in a compressed way,
introduced in TDS 7.3.B
Stream format url: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd304783.aspx

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_orderby
documentation:
Reads and processes ORDER stream

Used to inform client by which column dataset is ordered.
Stream format url: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd303317.aspx

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_end
documentation:
Reads and processes DONE/DONEINPROC/DONEPROC streams

Stream format urls:

- DONE: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd340421.aspx>
- DONEINPROC: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd340553.aspx>
- DONEPROC: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd340753.aspx>

```
param marker

Can be TDS_DONE_TOKEN or TDS_DONEINPROC_TOKEN or TDS_DONEPROC_TOKEN
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_env_chg
documentation:
Reads and processes ENVCHANGE stream.

Stream info url: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd303449.aspx

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_auth
documentation:
Reads and processes SSPI stream.

Stream info: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd302844.aspx

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.is_connected
documentation:
---
return: True if transport is connected
---

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.bad_stream
documentation:
Called when input stream contains unexpected data.

Will close stream and raise {class}`InterfaceError`
\:param msg: Message for InterfaceError exception.
\:return: Never returns, always raises exception.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.tds_version
documentation: Returns integer encoded current TDS protocol version

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.conn
documentation: Reference to owning {class}`_TdsSocket`

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.set_state
documentation:
Switches state of the TDS session.

It also does state transitions checks.
\:param state: New state, one of TDS_PENDING/TDS_READING/TDS_IDLE/TDS_DEAD/TDS_QUERING

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.querying_context
documentation:
Context manager for querying.

Sets state to TDS_QUERYING, and reverts it to TDS_IDLE if exception happens inside managed block,
and to TDS_PENDING if managed block succeeds and flushes buffer.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.make_param
documentation:
Generates instance of {class}`Param` from value and name

Value can also be of a special types:

- An instance of {class}`Param`, in which case it is just returned.
- An instance of {class}`output`, in which case parameter will become
  an output parameter.
- A singleton {var}`default`, in which case default value will be passed
  into a stored proc.

```
param name

Name of the parameter, will populate column_name property of returned column.

param value

Value of the parameter, also used to guess the type of parameter.

return

An instance of 
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession._convert_params
documentation:
Converts a dict of list of parameters into a list of {class}`Column` instances.

```
param parameters

Can be a list of parameter values, or a dict of parameter names to values.

return

A list of  instances.
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.cancel_if_pending
documentation:
Cancels current pending request.

Does nothing if no request is pending, otherwise sends cancel request,
and waits for response.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.submit_rpc
documentation:
Sends an RPC request.

This call will transition session into pending state.
If some operation is currently pending on the session, it will be
cancelled before sending this request.

Spec: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd357576.aspx>

```
param rpc_name

Name of the RPC to call, can be an instance of 

param params

Stored proc parameters, should be a list of  instances.

param flags

See spec for possible flags.
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.get_proc_outputs
documentation:
If stored procedure has result sets and OUTPUT parameters use this method
after you processed all result sets to get values of the OUTPUT parameters.
\:return: A list of output parameter values.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.get_proc_return_status
documentation:
Last executed stored procedure's return value

Returns integer value returned by `RETURN` statement from last executed stored procedure.
If no value was not returned or no stored procedure was executed return `None`.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.executemany
documentation: Execute same SQL query multiple times for each parameter set in the `params_seq` list.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.execute_scalar
documentation:
This method executes SQL query then returns first column of first row or the
result.

Query can be parametrized, see {func}`execute` method for details.

This method is useful if you want just a single value, as in:

```
conn.execute_scalar('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees')
```

This method works in the same way as `iter(conn).next()[0]`.
Remaining rows, if any, can still be iterated after calling this
method.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.submit_plain_query
documentation:
Sends a plain query to server.

This call will transition session into pending state.
If some operation is currently pending on the session, it will be
cancelled before sending this request.

Spec: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd358575.aspx>

```
param operation

A string representing sql statement.
```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.submit_bulk
documentation:
Sends insert bulk command.

Spec: <http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd358082.aspx>

```
param metadata

A list of  instances.

param rows

A collection of rows, each row is a collection of values.

return

```

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.put_cancel
documentation:
Sends a cancel request to the server.

Switches connection to IN_CANCEL state.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.rollback
documentation:
Rollback current transaction if it exists.
If `cont` parameter is set to true, new transaction will start immediately
after current transaction is rolled back

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.submit_rollback
documentation:
Send transaction rollback request.
If `cont` parameter is set to true, new transaction will start immediately
after current transaction is rolled back

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.begin_response
documentation:
Begins reading next response from server.

If timeout happens during reading of first packet will
send cancellation message.

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_tabname
documentation:
Processes TABNAME token

Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/openspecs/windows_protocols/ms-tds/140e3348-da08-409a-b6c3-f0fc9cee2d6e

methods.name: pytds.tds_session._TdsSession.process_colinfo
documentation:
Process COLNAME token

Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/openspecs/windows_protocols/ms-tds/aa8466c5-ca3d-48ca-a638-7c1becebe754

TDS session

This class has the following responsibilities:
* Track state of a single TDS session if MARS enabled there could be multiple TDS sessions
  within one connection.
* Provides API to send requests and receive responses
* Does serialization of requests and deserialization of responses

modules.name: pytds.tds_types
documentation: This module implements various data types supported by Microsoft SQL Server

functions.name: pytds.tds_types._decode_num
documentation:
Decodes little-endian integer from buffer

Buffer can be of any size

Partially length prefixed reader

Spec: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd340469.aspx

classes.name: pytds.tds_types.BaseTypeSerializer

methods.name: pytds.tds_types.BaseTypeSerializer.get_typeid
documentation: Returns type identifier of type.

methods.name: pytds.tds_types.BaseTypeSerializer.from_stream
documentation:
Class method that reads and returns a type instance.

```
param r

An instance of  to read type from.
```

Should be implemented in actual types.

methods.name: pytds.tds_types.BaseTypeSerializer.write_info
documentation:
Writes type info into w stream.

```
param w

An instance of  to write into.
```

Should be symmetrical to from_stream method.
Should be implemented in actual types.

methods.name: pytds.tds_types.BaseTypeSerializer.write
documentation:
Writes type's value into stream

```
param w

An instance of  to write into.

param value

A value to be stored, should be compatible with the type
```

Should be implemented in actual types.

methods.name: pytds.tds_types.BaseTypeSerializer.read
documentation:
Reads value from the stream.

```
param r

An instance of  to read value from.

return

A read value.
```

Should be implemented in actual types.

Base type for TDS data types.

All TDS types should derive from it.
In addition actual types should provide the following:

- type - class variable storing type identifier

classes.name: pytds.tds_types.BasePrimitiveTypeSerializer
documentation:
Base type for primitive TDS data types.

Primitive type is a fixed size type with no type arguments.
All primitive TDS types should derive from it.
In addition actual types should provide the following:

- type - class variable storing type identifier
- declaration - class variable storing name of sql type
- isntance - class variable storing instance of class

classes.name: pytds.tds_types.BaseTypeSerializerN
documentation:
Base type for nullable TDS data types.

All nullable TDS types should derive from it.
In addition actual types should provide the following:

- type - class variable storing type identifier
- subtypes - class variable storing dict {subtype_size: subtype_instance}

classes.name: pytds.tds_types.IntType
documentation:
Integer type, corresponds to `INT <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/data-types/int-bigint-smallint-and-tinyint-transact-sql>`_
type in the MSSQL server.

pytds.tds_types.Time.__init__
documentation:
Creates sql time object.
Maximum precision which sql server supports is 100 nanoseconds.
Values more precise than 100 nanoseconds will be truncated.
@param nsec: Nanoseconds from 00:00:00
